assertions.expectation: Recommends combining release channels with maintenance exclusions as the approach for maximum control
assertions.reason: Response explicitly recommends Extended channel combined with 'no minor or node upgrades' maintenance exclusion as the primary approach

assertions.expectation: Recommends 'no minor or node upgrades' exclusion to prevent disruptive changes while allowing control plane security patches. May also mention persistent exclusions with --add-maintenance-exclusion-until-end-of-support (no longer a 6-month max, auto-tracks EoS)
assertions.reason: Response specifically recommends 'no minor or node upgrades' exclusion and includes --add-maintenance-exclusion-until-end-of-support flag with explanation of persistent exclusion that auto-tracks End of Support

assertions.expectation: Recommends maintenance windows to control WHEN patches are applied (e.g., weekend windows only)
assertions.reason: Response includes maintenance window configuration with Saturday 2-6 AM window and provides gcloud commands with --maintenance-window-start, --maintenance-window-duration, and --maintenance-window-recurrence parameters

assertions.expectation: Mentions the cluster disruption budget (disruption interval) to control upgrade frequency — patch interval default 7d (max 90d), minor interval default 30d (max 90d)
assertions.reason: Response includes --maintenance-patch-version-disruption-interval=7776000s (90 days) and explicitly mentions 'Patch disruption interval: 90 days prevents back-to-back patches' with enforcement of minimum gap

assertions.expectation: Suggests Stable or Extended channel for production clusters in regulated environments. Extended channel provides maximum flexibility around EoS enforcement (additional cost only during extended period)
assertions.reason: Response recommends Extended channel specifically for financial services, mentions 24-month support vs 14-month standard, and explicitly notes additional cost only applies during extended period (months 15-24)

assertions.expectation: Notes that disabling control plane patches entirely is not recommended due to security risks — monthly patches at minimum
assertions.reason: Response emphasizes allowing security patches while blocking disruptive changes, states 'Patches still auto-applied for security' and maintains that control plane patches should continue automatically

assertions.expectation: May mention rollout sequencing as an advanced option for controlling upgrade order across multiple clusters, and/or the cluster disruption budget for controlling CP patch frequency
assertions.reason: Response mentions cluster disruption budget with 90-day patch disruption interval configuration, though rollout sequencing across multiple clusters is not explicitly covered

assertions.expectation: Provides gcloud commands for configuring the recommended exclusion type and maintenance window
assertions.reason: Response provides comprehensive gcloud commands including cluster update with release channel, maintenance exclusion scope, maintenance window configuration, and disruption interval settings
